ety: act. part. of قلّب, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Changing; turning, converting, &c.; — changer, &c.
more: part. act. (II. of قلب) Turning, turner, converter.
subs: The Converter of hearts, i.e. God.

ety: act. part. of قلّد 'to follow another in that which he says or does,' ii of قلد 'to twist, or wind, or wreathe'; but prob. formed fr. qilāda, q.v.
def: s.m. A follower (of another); an imitator; a disciple
more: (part. pass. II. of قلد) adj. Counterfeited, imitated.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Followed; imitated, mimicked; represented; counterfeited.

ety: act. part. of قامر 'to contend with (a person
def: s.m. An antagonist at a game of hazard; — a dice-player.
more: (part. act. III. of قمر) s. m. A player at dice.

ety: pass. part. of کمّل 'to complete,' &c., ii of کمل 'to be complete,' &c.
def: part. adj. Completed, perfected, made up; fitted with every requisite; finished; performed, accomplished; — complete, perfect, consummate, most excellent
more: adj. کمل Complete; perfect. nā-mukammal . adj. Incomplete.
(part. pass. II. of کمل) adj. Perfect, complete, fitted with every requisite, accomplished.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُکَمَّل (mukammal), from Arabic مُكَمَّل (mukammal). First attested in c. 1765 as Middle Hindi مکمل (mkml /⁠mukammal⁠/).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mukkā, and mūkā or mokā, qq.v. s.m. The fist; — a fist, a blow with the fist; thump, buffet
subs: v.n. The fist to be used; to come to blows
(-ko), To receive a blow with the fist; — to suffer a shock
(-ko), To strike a blow with the fist; to give (one) a buffet.

ety: pass. part. of اوقر 'to load; to oppress,' &c., iv of وقر 'to burden,' &c.
def: part. adj. Loaded; carrying a heavy burden; — pregnant (woman); — weighed down; oppressed; reduced to poverty or misery.
adj. & adv. (dialec.) corr. of مقرر muqarrar.

ety: for A. مقاربة, inf. n. of قارب, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Drawing near (to), approaching; approximation; — proximity; showing propinquity, or affinity.
more: (inf. III. of قرب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Approximation, showing affinity.

ety: for A. مقارنة, inf. n. of قارن, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Conjunction; connexion; companionship; familiarity; — proximity.
more: (inf. III. of قرن) s. f. Conjunction, connexion.

ety: mukar˚, prob. corr. of A. منکر munkar, 'denied,' or munkir, 'denying'
def: v.t. & v.n. To deny; to refuse; to go back from one's word; — to belie
more: v. n. A منکر See inkār k. , 1, 2, 4, 5. To deny; belie; go back from one's word. Bipat paṛī jib bheṭ manāī, mukar gayā jib denī āī . Rus. When trouble came a vow he made, when the trouble passed 'nay, nay' he said. (The devil was sick, the devil a saint would be; the devil got well, the devil a saint was he.)
(perhaps corrupt. of Arab. منکر) v. a. To deny.

ety: pass. part. of قرّب 'to cause to be near, or to approach or draw near,' ii of قرب 'to be or become near'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Caused to draw near (to); allowed to approach, admitted (to); — approximated; — nearly related; — one who is allowed to approach, or to have access (to a king, &c.); an intimate friend; a favourite; a relation; an attendant (pl. muqarrabīn)
more: n. m. An intimate friend or associate.

(part. pass. II. of قرب Heb. and Aram. ) 1. adj. Approximated, admitted. 2. (subst.) Intimate friend, favourite, relation, attendant. مقرب الحضرت muḳarrabu-l-ḥaẓrat, The kingʼs relations, blood royal. مقرب الخدمت muḳarrabu-l-ḵẖidmat, Confidential servant. (Pers. plur. مقربان muḳarrabān ).
subs: The relations of the king, blood royal
A confidential servant, or confidential servants.

ety: pass. part. of کرّم 'to honour, revere,' &c., ii of کرم 'to excel in honour,' &c.
def: part. adj. Honoured, respected, revered, venerable; noble, august, illustrious
more: adj. Noble; illustrious; respected; revered. mukarram-i-bandah , Honorable Sir.
(part. pass. II. of کرم) adj. Venerable, noble, illustrious, respected, revered.

ety: pass. part. of قرّر 'to settle, fix, establish, confirm,' &c., ii of قرّ 'to be settled,' &c.
def: part. adj. Settled, fixed, established, confirmed, ratified, agreed upon; appointed, assigned; constituted; determined, defined; prescribed; imposed; usual, customary; permanent; — ascertained, undoubted, certain; infallible, unquestionable; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, unquestionably, undoubtedly, positively, &c.
more: adj. قر 1. Fixed; established. 2. Appointed. 3. Certain. muqarrar ; Illit. mukar , adv. Assuredly; unquestionably ( zurūr , 2). Āj woh muqarrar āëṅge. muqarrar k. , v. a. 1. To set; settle ( taiyun k ). 2. To appoint; constitute. 3. To impose; levy (as a tax). ṭikas muqarrar k. , v. a. To impose or assess taxes. jamā muqarrar k. , v. a. To assess revenue. hissa muqarrar k. , v. a. To assign or allot shares.

(part. pass. II. of قرّ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Established, fixed, ascertained, certain, infallible, unquestionable; appointed, employed; adv. Certainly, assuredly, positively. مقررکرنا muḳarrar karnā, To fix, settle, establish, ascertain, secure, appoint, employ, appropriate, assign.
subs: v.t. To settle, fix, &c.; to appoint (to an office); to assign (a share, &c. to); to constitute, &c.; to impose (a tax, &c.); to assess (revenue).

ety: pass. part. of کرّر, ii (and intens.
def: part. Repeated, reiterated; — adv. Repeatedly; a second time, again
more: adv. کر repeating. Twice; again. mukarrar pesh k. , To present again; re-submit. mukarrar sikarrar , adv. A second and a third time; repeatedly; over and over again. mukarrar sikarrar kahnā . To press or urge repeatedly. mukarrar talab k. , G. G. To recall. mukarrar kahnā , v. n. To repeat; reiterate. mukarrar yeh hai, yā āṅki , A postscript.
(part. pass. II. of کرّ Heb. ) adj. Repeated; again. مکررکرنا mukarrar karnā, To repeat.
subs: adv. A second and a third time; repeatedly, over and over again
v.t. To tell or mention again, to repeat, reiterate

ety: for A. مقررة muqarrarat, fem. of muqarrar
def: part. adj. = muqarrar, q.v.; — s.m. A fixed allowance; appointed portion.
adj. 1. Fixed; established; determined; prescribed; laid down. 2. Defined. 3. Usual; customary. 4. Permanent. muqarrarah , n. m. A fixed allowance; appointed portion.

ety: fr. muqarrar
def: adj. Fixed; appointed, assigned; — s.f. A fixed tenure in perpetuity; a fixed lease; quit-rent; — a fixed stipend; — an appointment; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, &c.
more: n. f. 1. A fixed lease; quit rent. 2. A fixed stipend. muqarrarī jagah . Assigned places.
adj. Fixed, appointed. s. f. A fixed stipend, an appointment. adv. Certainly.
subs: s.m. A holder of a muqarrarī farm or tenure.

ety: pass. part. of قرّض, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Cut, clip, shredded (as with scissors).
more: (part. pass. II. of قرض Heb. ) adj. Cut, shredded (as with scissors).

ety: for A. مکاشفة mukāshafat, inf. n. of کاشف, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Disclosing, disclosure; displaying; manifestation; — revelation, apocalypse.
more: (inf. III. of کشف Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Displaying, disclosure.

ety: pass. part. of قشّر, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Peeled, rinded, barked, skinned, shelled, husked, &c.
more: (part. pass. II. of قشر) adj. Barked, skinned, peeled.

ety: pass. part. of قصّر, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Failing (of), coming short (of), deficient; defective.
more: (part. pass. II. of قصر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Deficient, defective.

ety: pass. part. of کسّر, ii, and intens. of کسر 'to break,' &c.
def: part. adj. Broken; — square; cubic; — s.m. Square measurement.

ety: act. part. of قسّم, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Dividing; distributing; — dispersing, scattering; — s.m. A divider, &c.; — muqassam (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Divided; distributed; — dispersed, scattered; — possessed of a beautiful (face); — beautified.

ety: for A. مکاتبة, inf. n. of کاتب 'to correspond by letter,' iii of کتب 'to write'
def: s.f. Corresponding by letter, epistolary correspondence.
more: (inf. III. of کتب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Corresponding by letters. (Plur. مکاتيب from مکتوب).

ety: for A. مقاتلة muqātalat, inf. n. of قاتل 'to try to kill; to fight with'; iii of قتل 'to kill'
def: s.m. Fighting (with); — battle, conflict, slaughter, carnage.
more: (inf. III. of قتل Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. Slaughter, carnage, battle.

ety: pass. part. of قطع 'to cut into several pieces; to dissect; to cut out,' &c., ii of قطع 'to cut'
def: part. adj. Cut into several pieces; cut down, reduced in size by cutting; short, dwarfish; — cut out, shaped; cut or trimmed according to the sharʻ (as a beard); — well-dressed; well-shaped, comely, beautiful; — solemn, serious (face or countenance).
more: adj. Solemn; serious. Kyā muqattā sūrat hai! muqatta ḍāṛhī , A beard cut according to the Mah. Shara .
wety: Borrowed from Persian مَقْطَع (maqta').

def: s.m. pl. (of muqat̤t̤aʻ), Small cuttings or cuts (of cloth), narrow strips (of cloth), narrow cloths; short verses or poems; verses; bits, parts or portions; texts (of Scripture).
more: s. m. plur. (of مقطع muḳat̤t̤aﻌ ) Small forms of verse or of cloth.

ety: act. part. of قطّر, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Dropping, dripping, trickling; distilling, exuding
more: part. act. قطر Dropped; dripped. Āb-i-muqattar .
part. act. (II. of قطر) Distilling, dropping. ¶ muḳat̤t̤ar (part. pass.) adj. Distilled.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Allowed to fall drop by drop; exuded in drops; distilled

ety: for A. مقاومة, inf. n. of قاوم 'to stand front to front with, to withstand, oppose,' &c., iii of قام (for قوم
def: s.f. Opposing, withstanding; opposition, resistance; competition
more: (inf. III. of قوم) s. m. Opposition, resistance, competition.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُقاوَمَت (muqāwamat), from Arabic مُقَاوَمَة (muqāwama).

ety: for A. مقوٍّ muqawwiṉ, act. part. of قوّي 'to strengthen,' &c., ii of قوِي 'to be strong,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Strengthening, invigorating, bracing; — confirming; corroborative; — a strengthener, &c. (pl. muqawwiyāt); — Ar. muqawwā (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Strengthened; confirmed; corroborated; — s.m. A box, or case (esp. one of a circular oblong shape in which rolls of paper are carried; — see muqābā)
more: adj. Strengthening; invigorating. muqqavvī-i-bāh , A specific for increasing venery; an aphrodisiac. muqavvī-i-badan , n. m. ( Med .) A restorative. muqavvī-i-dil , adj. ( Med .) Cardiac. muqavvī-i-dimāg , adj. Cephalic. muqavvī-i-kabid , adj. Hepatic. muqavvī-i-meda , n. m. A tonic; a stomachic.

(part. act. II. of قوي Heb. ) adj. Strengthening, invigorating. مقوي دل muḳauwī-i-dil, Cardiack. مقوي دماغ muḳauwī-i-dimāg̠ẖ, Cephalick. مقوي کبد muḳauwī-i-kabid, Hepatick. مقوي معده muḳauwī-i-miﻌda, Tonick.
subs: adj. & s.m. Aphrodisiac; stimulant; — an aphrodisiac, &c.
adj. & s.m. Cephalic; — a cephalic medicine
adj. & s.m. Tonic; — a tonic, a stomachic.

ety: act. part. of قوّم 'to make straight, or right,' &c., ii of قام (for قوم
def: part. adj. & s.m. Making straight, setting right; rightly disposing; rectifying, adjusting, arranging; directing; fixing, constituting; — fixing a price; — one who sets right, or rectifies, &c.
more: (part. act. II. of قوم) adj. Rightly disposing, adjusting, directing, fixing a price.

ety: act. part. of اقبل 'to look towards,' &c., iv of قبل 'to look towards the tip of the nose'
def: adj. Looking towards; advancing towards, approaching; inclining towards, favouring (fortune); — prosperous, fortunate.
more: (part. act. IV. of قبل) adj. Inclining towards, looking towards; favouring.

def: adj. & s.m. (Dakh.) = mukdhā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (Dakh.) Ignorant, stupid; — dumb, mute; — an ignorant (or a stupid) person, a simpleton, &c. (cf. the Marāthī mukā).

ety: S. मुग्ध+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (Dakh.) Ignorant, stupid; — dumb, mute; — an ignorant (or a stupid) person, a simpleton, &c. (cf. the Marāthī mukā).

ety: S. मुख+कीला]
def: s.f. (dialec.) = nakel, q.v. s.f. The wooden or iron pin fixed in a camel's nose, and to which his halter is fastened; a cavesson; — the halter by which a camel is led, and which is fastened to a pin through his nose.
s.m. (dialec.) = مکهہ mukh, q.v.
s.m. The mouth; face, countenance (i.q. muṅh, q.v.); — beak (of a bird); snout, or muzzle (of an animal); — front, fore-part; head, prow (of a boat); — aperture; entrance; commencement
more: s. m. The mouth, the face. مکهہ بيڙ mukh-bheṛ, adj. (dakh.) Opposite, in front: see مقابل muḳābil.
subs: (˚kha+ar˚), adj. Lotus-faced; an epithet of the face or countenance of any deity
(˚kha+ag˚), s.m. A sort of goblin with a face of fire; — fire put into the mouth of a corpse at the time of lighting the funeral pile; — a forest-conflagration; — a sacrificial or consecrated fire
or mukh-bheṛ, adv. Before the face (of, -ke), in front (of), facing, confronting, opposite (syn. muqābil)
s.m. Filling the mouth; a mouthful
s.m. 'Face-moon'; a moon-like face, a lovely face
(-meṅ), To set the face (towards); to tend (to)
(-kī or), To turn the face (to, or towards); to face
s.m. Cleaning the face; any substance for cleaning the face
vulg. mukh-baćan, s.m. Word of mouth; report, rumour, tradition
s.m. Colourlessness of face; change of countenance.

def: n. m. S. मुख The mouth; face (منه 1). 1. Jī kā dukh dikhāve mukh . Rus. Prov. The heart's woe the face doth show. 2. Ho chhail! mohe mukhṛā māṅjan de re! Mukhṛā māṅjat besar uljhī, mohe lat suljhāvan de re! Rus. Wom. Song. My wanton love! leave me alone to brush my teeth just, My nose-pin is displaced, my hair all tossed, I dress must. 3. Yād āvat unke mukh kī batyāṅ more kasak uṭhat jiyā meṅ . Whene'er I dwell on words his lips expressed my heart doth throb anew within my breast. mukh, mukkh, mukhiyā , adj. Principal; chief; paramount. mukh-tāl' , n. m. A chorus. mukh chūmnā , v. n. To kiss. mukh mantarī , n. m. Prime minister. mukhā'gar , adv. Face to face. mukher'ā, makherā , A muzzle. mukh'iyā , n. m. A head; leader; principal.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मुख (mukha). Doublet of مُن٘ہ (mũh).

ety: corr. of mukhya, q.v.
def: adj. Principal, chief, &c.
prop. n. The town of Mocha (on the west coast of Arabia).
more: n. prop. Mocha, a town on the sea coast of Arabia.

ety: pass. part. of خبّط, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Disordered in the intellect, rendered mad or foolish, demented, insane.
more: (part. pass. II. of خبط) adj. Insane, rendered foolish.

ety: pass. part. of خدّر 'to conceal; to benumb,' &c., ii of خدر 'to keep oneself behind a curtain; to be benumbed'
def: part. Kept behind a curtain or a veil; veiled; concealed
more: (part. pass. II. of خدر) adj. Concealed, kept behind a veil.
subs: (vulg.) muḵẖdir (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Benumbing; narcotic.

ety: pl. of muḵẖaddir
def: s.m. Narcotics; anodynes.

ety: pass. part. of خفّف 'to make or render light,' &c. ii of خفّ 'to be or become light'
def: part. adj. Made light, lightened; alleviated, abated; contracted; abbreviated; (a consonant) having a short for a long vowel, or a tashdīd removed; — light; contemptible; — s.m. A contracted form, a contraction (as ṣ for ṣāḥīḥ).
more: adj. خف was light. Contracted; abbreviated. mukhaffaf , n. m. A contraction, as ص for sahīh right.
(part. pass. II. of خفّ Chald. ) adj. 1. Alleviated, abated. 2. Light. 3. Contemptible. 4. (A consonant) having a short for a long vowel.

ety: for A. مخففة muḵẖaffafat, fem. of muḵẖaffaf
def: part. = muḵẖaffaf, q.v. part. adj. Made light, lightened; alleviated, abated; contracted; abbreviated; (a consonant) having a short for a long vowel, or a tashdīd removed; — light; contemptible; — s.m. A contracted form, a contraction (as ṣ for ṣāḥīḥ).

ety: S. मुख + अग्रे
def: adv. (dialec.) Before the face (of, -ke), face to face (with), in the presence (of).

ety: pass. part. of خيّم 'to pitch a tent,' ii of خيم; but formed fr. ḵẖaima, q.v.
def: part. Tented; — s.m. An encampment, a camp.

ety: act. part. of خيّل 'to imagine, fancy,' ii of خال (for خيل
def: part. adj. & s.m. Imagining, fancying; doubting; being deceived; — conjecturing from physiognomy; — one who imagines, &c.
more: part. act. (II. of خيل Heb. ) Imagining, fancying, doubting, being deceived, conjecturing from physiognomy.

ety: act. part. of خيّر 'to give (one
def: part. adj. Giving an option or choice (of two or more things); — bountiful, charitable, liberal
more: (part. act. II. of خير) adj. 1. Bountiful, charitable, liberal. 2. Left to free choice, indifferent, giving an option of two things.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Left to free choice; — indifferent.

ety: pass. part. of خجّل 'to cause to be ashamed,' &c., ii. of خجل 'to be or become ashamed'
def: part. Put to shame, made ashamed; ashamed; confounded; bashful.
(part. pass. II. of خجل) adj. Confounded, bashful, ashamed.

ety: for A. مخالفة, inf. n. of خالف 'to disagree with; to oppose,' &c., iii of خلف 'to follow,' &c.
def: s.f. Opposition, contrariety; repugnance; contradiction; contravention; transgression, disobedience, rebellion; disagreement, dissension, variance, discord; — enmity, hostility
more: n. f. 1. Enmity; opposition; contrariety; hostility. 2. Disagreement; dissension. mukhālifat k. , v. a. To oppose; go against.
(inf. III. of خلف) s. f. Opposition, dissension, adversity, repugnance, variance, enmity. مخالفت کرنا muḵẖālafat karnā, To oppose.
subs: (-kī), To oppose; to dissent (from); to contradict; to confute; to resist, withstand, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُخَالِفَت (muxālifat), borrowed from Arabic مُخَالَفَة (muḵālafa), from خَالَفَ (ḵālafa).

ety: for A. مخاالطة, inf. n. of خالط 'to mix or mingle with,' iii of خلط 'to mix, mingle'
def: s.f. Mixing together in society; conversing.
more: (inf. III. of خلط) s. f. Mixing together in society.

ety: act. part. of خالف; see next
def: adj. & s.m. Contrary, opposite, adverse; unfavourable, unsuitable, uncongenial; repugnant, dissentient; contradictory; — an opponent, adversary, enemy, foe
more: n. m. خلف coming after. An opponent; adversary. mukhālif , adj. Opposite; adverse; contrary. mukhālif h. , v. n. 1. To oppose. 2. To contravene.
(part. act. III. of خلف) adj. Opposite, adverse, contrary, dissentient; (subst.) An opponent, adversary, enemy, foe.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُخَالِف (muxālif), borrowed from Arabic مُخَالِف (muḵālif), from خَالَفَ (ḵālafa). First attested in c. 1528 as Middle Hindi مخالف (mxalf /⁠muxālif⁠/).

ety: for A. مخَلي muḵẖallā, pass. part. of خلّي 'to leave (a place
def: part. adj. Empty; — dismissed, liberated, set free, left at liberty, let alone; repudiated
more: (prop. مخلايٰ part. pass. II. of خلو Heb. ) adj. 1. Empty. 2. Dismissed, set at liberty, repudiated, set free. مخلا با لطبع muḵẖallā bi-t̤-t̤abﻌ, Unceremonious.
subs: adj. Unceremonious, unrestrained, easy, free; free and easy.

ety: pass. part. of خلّع, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Presented with a ḵẖilʻat; dressed or robed in a ḵẖilʻat.

ety: pass. part. of خلّد 'to cause to remain,' &c., ii of خلد 'to remain,' &c.
def: adj. Eternal, durable, permanent.
more: (part. pass. II. of خلد Heb. ) adj. Eternal, durable, permanent.

ety: pass. part. of خمّس 'to make (a thing
def: adj. Quintupled; five-cornered, five-angled, pentangular; — s.m. A pentagon; — a kind of verse of five lines.
more: n. m. خمس five. 1. A pentagon ( pachkoniyā ). 2. A verse consisting of five lines. mukhammas , adj. Quintupled; pentangular.
(part. pass. II. of خمس Heb. ) 1. s. m. A kind of verse containing five lines, a pentagon. 2. adj. Quintupled, pentangular. (Plur. مخمسات muḵẖammasāt ).

def: s.m. pl. of muḵẖammas, q.v.

ety: act. part. of خمّر, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Leavening; — one who leavens; a baker.
more: (part. act. IV. of خمر Heb. ) adj. Fermented, leavened. ¶ muḵẖammir (part. act. II.) adj. Leavening.

def: s.m. pl. (in Braj) of mukh, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit मक्खण (makkhaṇa), from Sanskrit म्रक्षण (mrakṣaṇa).

ety: fr. S. मुख or मुप्व्य
def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Leading, principal, chief; possessing authority or power; — powerful, able, strong, mighty; violent; — leader, leading person, chief; a grandee; — a bully.
more: adj. (dakh.) Powerful, strong, violent, a bully ( Harris ).

ety: -panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Vedic त्वन)+कं
def: s.m. Powerfulness, ability, power, might, &c.

ety: pass. part. of خنّث 'to make soft or effeminate,' ii of خنث 'to be soft or effeminate'
def: adj. & s.m. Effeminate; — abject, infamous; — an hermaphrodite; — a catamite
more: adj. Effeminate. mukhannas , n. m. An hermaphrodite. mukhannas k. , v. a. To emasculate.
(part. pass. II. of خنث) adj. Effeminate, abject, infamous; (subst.) 1. An hermaphrodite. 2. Exoletus, qui corpus prostituit cinædis (quod semet inflectit. Gol. ).

ety: mukar(nā), q.v.+ī = Prk. एअव्वअइअ = S. aff. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. A kind of short poem (or a kind of riddle in verse), of frequent use in the Braj dialect (it consists of four lines, each composed of four trochees; in the first three, the speaker (a woman) appears to talk of her lover; but, on the question being put by a friend, applies the whole to some other object; e.g. Bāṭ ćalat miro aṅćrā gahe, Merī sune na apnī kahe: Nā kaćhhu mo soṅ jhagṛā jhāṅṭā; — Kyūṅ sakhī! sajjan? nā sakhī, kāṅṭā: 'As I went along the road he seized my robe; Neither heard what I said, nor made any reply; Nor was there any quarrelling or wrangling with me; — How, friend! your lover? No, my dear, a thorn')
more: n. f. A kind of Riddle in verse, so called from mukarnā to deny. The first three lines are a double entendre , and the fourth line consists of the answer which they suggest, viz. 'A lover,' and its repudiation by the questioner, who then names the other word signified by the double entendre . Amīr Khusro is said to have been the inventor of the Mukrī .

s. f. A kind of short poem of frequent use in the Braj dialect, the peculiarities of which are these. It consists of four lines, each composed of four trochees. In the three first the speaker, a female, appears to talk of her lover; but, on the question being put by a friend, applies the whole to some other object. Hence the name from mukarnā, To deny. The following is an example. باٿ چلت مروانچراگہي ميري سني نه اپني کہي ناکچهہ موسون جهگڙا جهانٿا کيون سکهي سجن ناسکهي کانٿا Bāṭ chalat miro anchrā gahe; merī sune na apnī kahe. Nā kachhu mo soṅ jhagṛā jhānṭā; kyūṅ sakhī sajjan? nā sakhī kānṭā. As I went along the road, he seized my robe; neither heard what I said, nor made any reply. In short we had not the smallest dispute. Who, friend, your lover? no, friend, a thorn.

Manne kar le nār, mukat hogī; jo tauṅ manne karṭī nāhīṅ tan teri kimaiṅ nā pat hogī . Rus. Keep me, my love! salvation you shall have. If you refuse to keep me now in no respect you will be held. mukt-dātā , n. m. A. deliverer; Saviour.

def: s.f. contrac. of مکتي mukti, q.v.
s.f. A pearl (as loosened or set free from the pearl-oyster shell)
more: adj. 1. Much; enough ( kāfī ). Sauk mare to lāḍū bāṅṭūṅ, aur dān dhan dūn muḳtā, Pachpīrāṅ kī karūṅ kaḍhāī, us māṅ ghī ghālūṅ muktā . Rus. Wom. song. If but my co-wife dies I'll sweets and alms give much, For the five saints set on the pot, and butter put in much. 2. Many; a great many. Anḍe hoṅge to bachche mukte ho raheṅye . Rus. Prov. If you have the eggs you will have, plenty of chickens.

A pearl ( motī ). 1. Nak muktā bidrum bhayo op adhar kī pōë, Nain sain gunjā bhayo, haṅs muktā ho jāë . Bīhārī Satsai. Her red lips made the pearl hung from her nose red seem, Then abrus black-tipped turned beneath her black eyes' gleam, But when she laughed you saw again the pearl's soft beam. 2. Gaj muktā bānar līe, Bed līye aggyān, Param sundar yogi līe, kāyar hāth kamāṅ . Doha. To a donkey the Veds, to a monkey a pearl, To a coward a bow, to a friar a girl!
n. f. A silken dhotī , q. v.
s. m. A pearl. Also مکتا پهل मुक्ताफल muktā-phal, A pearl.
subs: s.m. A pearl; — camphor; — a custard-apple, Annona squamosa; — fine writing; a fine hand; calligraphy
or muktāvali (˚tā+āw˚), s.f. Pearl necklace; — name of various books
s.m. A string of pearls; a pearl necklace.

ety: S. मूलं
def: s.m. Root (of a plant or tree); a root (lit. and met.); basis, groundwork, beginning, principle, origin, source, cause; — bottom (of anything); foot of a mountain, &c.); lower part, or end; basis, foundation; — end, or juncture (of anything); — generation, race, stock; — original property, capital, principal, stock-in-trade (syn. aṣl); — original, original text (of any work, as distinguished from the ṭīkā, or commentary); — (in Arith.) the square root (i.q. barg-mūl; syn. jaẕr); — the twenty-fourth (or, according to some, the seventeenth, or the nineteenth) Nakshatra or lunar asterism (containing eleven stars, which appear to be the same as those in the tail of Scorpio, and are considered unlucky); — adj. & adv. Radical; original; proper; real; — radically; really
more: n. m. S. मूलम् root. See asl , 1, 3, 8, 9. 1. Stock or capital. Biyāj to chhoṛā, par mūl to de! 2. Text; the original text; context. 3. ( Arith .) The square root ( jazr ). 4. The 24th, or according to some, the 17th or 19th lunar asterism containing eleyen stars, considered unlucky. Ye laṛkā mūl meṅ janmā hai . mūl-patr , n. m. An original document. mūl gāoṅ . The original or old established village. mūl'iyā , n. m. 1. One who is born while the moon is in Scorpionis , considered unlucky. Mūliyā mā bāp pe bhārī hotā hai . 2. ( bhaḍṛī ) A soothsayer; fortune-teller. 3. (Brij) A crafty person.
s. m. 1. Origin, root, race, generation, principal or capital sum of money. 2. Text (of a book, opposed to comment). 3. Name of the nineteenth lunar mansion ( ν or υ Scorpionis). 4. adj. Really.
subs: s.m. The basis or text of a lesson, or lecture, or discourse
s.m. Original deed, document, or writing
or mūl-dhan, s.m. Original property; capital, principal, stock (syn. aṣluʼl-māl)
s.m. The essence, or drift, of wisdom
s.f. Equality with, or resemblance to, the root or origin
s.m. Connexion with the root, origin, or source
s.m. (in Gram.) A primary or simple vowel
s.m. The author of an original work; — an epithet of the Great creator
s.m. The original, or old-established, village; — the native village of the founder of a race or family
adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing roots; possessing edible roots; — practising magic with roots (?).

wety: Causative of ملنا (milnā).

def: s.m. Courtesy, politeness; benignity, favour, kindness; — a letter, an epistle.
more: (inf. III. of لطف) s. m. 1. Courtesy, kindness, politeness, favour. 2. A letter or epistle.

def: s.f. = mulahṭī, or mulhaṭṭī, q.v.
s.m. The state or condition of a root, the being a root; the having root, foundation, or source (in anything); the being occasioned (by), or due (to), or arising (from anything).

ety: pass. part. of لوّن 'to colour,' &c., ii of لان (for لون
def: part. adj. Coloured; of various colours, variegated.
more: (part. pass. II. of لون) adj. Coloured, variegated.

ety: pass. part. of لوّث 'to be foul,' &c., ii of لاث (for لوث
def: part. adj. Befouled, polluted, contaminated.
more: (part. pass. II. of لوث Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Polluted.
wety: From Arabic مُلَوَّث (mulawwaṯ).

ety: for A. ملآءَمة mulāʼamat, inf. n. of لآءَم 'to be concordant; to agree (with
def: s.f. Softness; tenderness; smoothness; gentleness; affability; meekness; tractability.
more: (inf. III. of لأم) s. f. Softness, tenderness, gentleness, affability.

ety: for A. ملازمة, inf. n. of لازم, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Assiduity, diligence; close attendance (on); waiting (on); service, duty; paying respects (to a superior)
more: n. f. 1. Attendance; service; duty. 2. Waiting on a superior. mulāzimat ikhtiyār k. , v. a. To take service. mulāzimat hāsil k. , v. n. To be permitted to wait upon; to be admitted to an audience.
(inf. III. of لزم) s. f. 1. Assiduity, diligence. 2. Service, ( met. ) paying respects to a superiour, attendance.
subs: (-kī), To take service (with), enter the service (of)
(-kī), To be admitted to an audience; to be permitted to wait (upon), or to pay (one's) respects (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُلَازَمَت (mulāzamat), from Arabic مُلَازَمَة (mulāzama).

ety: act. part. of لازم; see mulāzamat
def: adj. & s.m. Assiduous, diligent; attentive; attached (to); adhering firmly (to); persevering; inseparable; — a servant, an attendant (upon); a follower
more: u. m. لزم being necessary. A servant; follower; an attendant. Mulāzim-i-nau tez-rau . P. Prov. A new broom sweeps clean. mulāzim-i-khās , A private servant. mulāzim khāngī , n. m. Domestic servants; domestics. mulāzim-i-sarkārī , n. m. G. G. A Govt. or public servant. mulāzim-i-sarkārī bannā , G. G. Personating a public servant. mulāzim k., yā banānā , v. a. To engage a servant.
(part. act. III. of لزم) adj. 1. Assiduous, diligent, attentive, attached to. 2. (subst.) A servant, attendant. (Pers. plur. ملازمان).
subs: A government servant, a public servant
To become a public servant; to personate a public servant
(-ko), To make a servant or attendant of; to engage a servant.

def: s.m. pl. of mulāzim, q.v.

ety: fr. mulāzim, q.v.
def: s.f. Attendance; service (i.q. mulāzamat).

ety: a post-classical formation fr. laẕẕat, q.v.
def: adj. Delicious (cf. laẕīẕ).
more: (part. pass. II. of لذّ) adj. Delicious.

ety: pass. part. of الحق, iv of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Added, joined, annexed, appended; — attributed; — contiguous, adjoining (syn. milā-huʼā); adjunct; adhering, adherent; coherent; belonging (to); correlative; — adopted
more: adj. Annexed (متصل). mulhiq h. , v. n. To be annexed.
(part. pass. IV. of لحق) adj. Joined, added, annexed, adhering, adjunct.
subs: (-se), To be added, or annexed, &c. (to); to be contiguous, or adjoining (to), &c.

ety: for A. ملحقة mulḥaqat, fem. of mulhaq
def: part. adj. = mulḥaq, q.v.; — s.m. What is annexed, or appended, &c.; an appendage; an appendix.
s. f. Name of a medicinal root, liquorice.

ety: Prk. महुलट्ठिआ; S. मधु+यष्टिका
def: s.f. Stick-liquorice, liquorice.
(for A. ملهٍ mulhiṉ, act. part. of اَلْہَي 'to amuse,' &c., iv of لًہا (for لہو) 'to play, to amuse ooneself,' &c.), One who affords amusement; a mimic, buffoon, jester.
more: (part. act. IV. of لہو) s. m. A jester, mimick, buffoon.

ety: act. part. of الحد, iv of (and syn. with
def: s.m. One who deviates or departs from the true faith; a heretic; a pagan, an unbeliever; one who denies the resurrection of the dead; a deist (pl. mulḥidīn).
more: n. m. An infidel ( kāfir ); a free thinker.
(part. act. IV. of لحد) s. m. A heretick, unbeliever, one who denies the resurrection, a deist, an apostate. (Arab. plur. ملحدين mulḥidīn ).

ety: act. part. of الہم 'to inspire,' &c., iv of لہم 'to swallow,' &c.
def: s.m. Inspiring; — inspirer
more: part. act. (IV. of لہم Heb. ) Inspiring, inspirer. ¶ mulham, part. pass. Inspired.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Inspired.

ety: for A. ملہمة mulhimat, fem. of mulhim
def: part. adj. = mulhim.

ety: S. मूलिका
def: s.f. A radish, Raphanus sativus; — (met.) anything worthless or good for nothing.
more: n. f. S. मूलक 1. Radish. Naī aur naram mūlī hai achār ko! Street cry. 2. Anything worthless or good for nothing.

ety: S. मूलिकः
def: adj. & s.m. Born (or one who is born) when the moon is in the asterism Mūl (ν or υ Scorpionis, which is considered unlucky); — mūliyā, or moliyā (dialec.) adj. & s.m. Stupid; — a stupid fellow, a blockhead.
more: adj. Born while the moon is in mūl ( ν or υ Scorpionis, which is considered unlucky).

ety: inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. Dominion, sovereignty, rule, reign; a kingdom, realm, state, country, territory, region, land
more: n. m. A conntry; state; territory. Mulk-i-Khudā taṅg nest, pāë marā laṅg nest . Prov. God's universe is wide enough, no helpless cripple am I. mulk se khārij k .; H. des-nikālā denā , v. a. To banish; exile. mulk-gīrī , n. f. Conquest; territorial conquest; annexation.

s. m. A country, kingdom, realm, region, reign, sovereignty, dominion. ملک داري mulk-dār-ī, s. f. Dominion, empire, government, ruling. ملک راني mulk-rān-ī (ران rān, part. act. of راندن To drive) s. f. Sovereignty, absolute sway. ملک ستاني mulk-sitān-ī, s. f. Conquest. ملک گيري mulk-gīr-ī, s. f. Conquest, territorial conquest, sovereignty, political affairs. ¶ malak (Heb. ) s. m. An angel. ملک الموت malaku-l-maut, The angel of death, Iﻌzrāʼīl. ¶ milk, s. f. Possession, dominion, property, right. (Plur. املاک). ¶ malik (Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A king. (Plur. ملوک). ملک الشعرا maliku-sh-shuʼaﻌrā, Prince of poets. ملک زاده malik-zāda, s. m. A prince, a kingʼs son.
subs: s.f. Ruling, rule, sway, government, dominion, empire
s.f. Carrying on the government (of), ruling; rule, sovereignty, absolute sway
s.f. Taking of a kingdom or kingdoms; conquest
(-ko), To expel from a country, to banish, exile
s.f. Conquest, territorial conquest, annexation; sovereignty; political affairs; — progress of a sovereign through his dominions.
ex: نِیُوزی لَین٘ڈ اَور بَرْطانیَہ بَہُت خوش حال مَمالِک ہَیں۔
niyūzī-laiṇḍ aur bartānya bahot xuś-hāl mamālik ha͠i.
New Zealand and the UK are very prosperous countries.
کَئی مُلْکوں میں مَذْہَب کی آزادی فَراہِم نَہِیں ہَے۔
kaī mulkõ mẽ mazhab kī āzādī farāhim nahī̃ hai.
There are many countries where there is no freedom of religion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُلْک (mulk), from Arabic مُلْك (mulk). First attested as Middle Hindi ملک (mulk).

def: s.m. (dialec.) The part of the bodice which covers the breasts.

ety: for A. ملکة, fr. mulk
def: s.f. Possession (i.q. milkat); — kingdom, &c. (i.q. mulk, q.v.). s.f. Possession, property.
more: n. m. The part of the bodice which covers the breasts.

ety: rel. n. fr. mulk, q.v.
def: adj. Of, or belonging to, or relating to, empire or government; imperial, royal; — political; civil; pertaining to the country; of or belonging to one's own country; national, indigenous; native; domestic; — s.f. An era in use in some places (as in Puranyā), which is one month in advance of the Faṣlī year (beginning with the first of Sāwan)
more: adj. 1. Belonging to one's country; national; provincial. 2. Political; civil. 3. Native; domestic. mulkī , n. f. An era in use in some places, as in Puranyā , which is one month in advance of the Faslī year, beginning with the 1st of Sāvan . mulkī lāṭh , n. m. The Viceroy or Governor General.
subs: s.m. 'The civil Lord or Governor,' the Viceroy, Governor-General.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملکی (mulkī), from Arabic مُلْكِيّ (mulkiyy). Equivalent to مُلْک (mulk, “country”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: i.q. mullā, q.v.
def: s.m. A decoy-bird.

ety: corr. of A. مَولَی maulā; cf. maulawī
def: s.m. A doctor, a learned man; a schoolmaster (who, in a village, has also charge of the mosque); — a judge, the deputy of a Qāzī; a jurist; — a priest; — a bigot; — (dialec.) a soothsayer, a fortune-teller; — mullā-qǒrʼānī, s.m. A law-officer who administers oaths on the Qorʼān; — one whose business it is to read the Qorʼān for forty days at the house of a deceased person
more: n. m. Cor. of مولیٰ 1. A doctor; professor. 2. A Mah. jurist; parish priest. 1. Mullā na hogā to kyā masjid meṅ azāṅ na hogī? . Prov. If there's no priest is there to be no worship? 2. Mullāṅ jī kyā kaheṅ ākhūṅ jī pahle hī samjhe hue haiṅ . Prov. What new thing can the priest say that the tutor knew not before? 3. Bed kiteb paṛheṅ, wai khutba, wai mullā, wai Pānḍe, Begar begar nām dharāë, ek māṭī ke bhānḍe, Waī khassī, wai gāë kaṭāvahīṅ, bādhī janam gaṅvāyā, Kahe Kabīr ye donoṅ bhūle, Rām kīṅhu na pāyā . Kabīr. The Mulla reads the Alcorān, the Pānde reads the Ved, By different names though them you style of one clay both are made, In slaught'ring ever goats or cows alike they waste their days, Kabīr says, 'neither e'er sees God, each from the right path strays.' 3. A judge; the deputy of a Qāzī. 4. A Mah. village schoolmaster who has also charge of the village mosque, and sometimes acts …
s. m. 1. (corrupt. of موليٰ maulạ̄ ) A schoolmaster, a doctor. 2. (corrupt. of ملواح) A decoy-bird. 3. adj. Learned (in Arabic).
subs: s.f. The office or duty of a mullā; schoolmastering, teaching, &c.

ety: corr. of A. ملواح milwāḥ
def: s.m. A decoy-bird.
s. f. Office or profession of mullā, q. v. doctorship.
more: s. f. Cream. (Cream or milk inspissated by boiling. Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: mullā, q.v.+ī = S. इन् and इ
def: adj. Of or relating to a mullā; — s.f. Office or profession of a mullā; doctorship; teaching (see mullā-garī).

s.m. corr. of mullā, q.v. s.m. A doctor, a learned man; a schoolmaster (who, in a village, has also charge of the mosque); — a judge, the deputy of a Qāzī; a jurist; — a priest; — a bigot; — (dialec.) a soothsayer, a fortune-teller; — mullā-qǒrʼānī, s.m. A law-officer who administers oaths on the Qorʼān; — one whose business it is to read the Qorʼān for forty days at the house of a deceased person
more: (from ملا mullā, q. v.) s. m. (dakh.) A fortuneteller ( Harris ).
subs: s.f. The office or duty of a mullā; schoolmastering, teaching, &c.

ety: mullā, q.v.+S. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a mullā; a learned woman; a school-mistress.
more: s. f. The wife of a Mullā, a learned woman, a schoolmistress.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = maulsarī.

ety: act. part. of التفت 'to turn oneself (to or towards another
def: part. adj. Turning (to or towards), paying regard (to); giving attention (to); attentive (to); having regard (for), showing respect (to); — inclined (towards); turning the head to look (at); leaning, or looking back; turning the head aside
more: (part. act. VIII. of لفت Heb. ) adj. Paying regard to, shewing respect to, turning the head to look (at), inclined (towards), attentive (to), having regard.
subs: (-kī t̤araf), To turn (to or towards); to pay regard (to), to attend (to), &c.

ety: n. of place fr. التجأ, viii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. A place of refuge; an asylum; a protector.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of لجأ) s. m. An asylum, a protector.

ety: act. part. of التجأ; see multajā
def: part. adj. & s.m. Fleeing (to) for refuge, taking refuge (with); having recourse (to); — one who flies (to), or takes refuge (with); a refugee; a supplicant, petitioner.
more: part. act. (VIII. of لجأ) Taking refuge with or having recourse to.

ety: act. part. of التمس, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Petitioning, supplicating, begging, praying, beseeching; — petitioner, &c.
more: Ped. n. m. لمس touching. A petitioner.
part. act. (VIII. of لمس) Begging, praying, supplicating.
subs: (-kā), To be a petitioner, &c. (for); to beg, pray, supplicate.

ety: S. मूल+स्थान+इकः or ईयः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Multān; produced in, or coming from, Multān; — a native or an inhabitant of Multān; — (S. mūla+sthāna+ikā), s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode
more: n. f. lit . earth of Multān. Yellow ochre.
1. s. f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode. 2. adj. Of Multān. ملتاني مٿي multānī maṭṭī, s. f. Armenian bole. ( Lit. earth of Multān).
subs: or multānī maṭṭī or miṭṭī, s.f. 'Multān earth'; Armenian bole; yellow ochre.
wety: From ملتان (multān, “Multan”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: for A. ملتوٍ multawiṉ, act. part. of التوَي 'to be bent, or twisted, or distorted,' &c., viii of لوَي 'to bend,' &c.
def: part. adj. Bent, crooked; oblique; distorted; — delayed, protracted; postponed, adjourned; pending
more: adj. Postponed; adjourned; pending. multavī rakhnā, yā k. , v. a. 1. To suspend; intermit. 2. To postpone; adjourn; defer; put off. multavī rahnā, yā h. , v. n. To be deferred, left in abeyance.
(part. act. VIII. of لوي Heb. ) adj. Bent, crooked; delayed, postponed, spinning out, protracted.
subs: or multawī karnā, v.t. To delay; to postpone, adjourn, defer, put off; to keep in abeyance; to suspend; to intermit
v.n. To be delayed, or postponed, &c.; to remain, or be left, in abeyance.

ety: act. part. of التزم 'to be made assiduous; to be constrained, or compelled,' &c., viii of لزِم 'to be assiduous,' &c.
def: part. adj. Constrained, compelled; — convicted; — conjunct; necessarily following; attached (to).
more: (part. act. VIII. of لزم) adj. Constrained, compelled, necessarily following, convicted.

def: s.m. A trumpet made of a horn (used by faqīrs); — small bells used as music.
s.m. pl. (of malik), Kings.
s.m. corr. 1˚ of mulk, q.v.; — 2˚ of mulūk, q.v.
ex: نِیُوزی لَین٘ڈ اَور بَرْطانیَہ بَہُت خوش حال مَمالِک ہَیں۔
niyūzī-laiṇḍ aur bartānya bahot xuś-hāl mamālik ha͠i.
New Zealand and the UK are very prosperous countries.
کَئی مُلْکوں میں مَذْہَب کی آزادی فَراہِم نَہِیں ہَے۔
kaī mulkõ mẽ mazhab kī āzādī farāhim nahī̃ hai.
There are many countries where there is no freedom of religion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُلْک (mulk), from Arabic مُلْك (mulk). First attested as Middle Hindi ملک (mulk).

ety: mulūk, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Royal, princely; like a king; fit for a king.
s.m. Price, &c. (i.q. mol, q.v.); wages; hire. s.m. Purchase; — original value; value, price, worth, purchase-money
subs: or mol ćaṛhānā (-kā), To enhance, or raise, the price (of); — to bid against (one, at an auction, &c.); to outbid
(-kā), To beat down, or to abate, the price (of)
s.m. Price (indefinitely); price and cost; — fixing the price, or value (of, -kā), valuation, appraisement; — bargaining; purchasing; trading; traffic
or mol-tol ćukānā, or mol-tol karnā (-kā), To fix or settle the price, or value (of), to value, appraise, estimate; to bargain (for)
mol ṭhahrānā or ćukānā or karnā
v.t. 'To give for a price,' to sell (to, -ko)
(-kā), To reduce, or to beat down, the price (of)
(-kā), To value, appraise, &c. (i.q. mol-tol ṭhahrānā); to make an offer (for)
(-ko), To purchase and set free, or to purchase (a slave, &c.) for the purpose of setting (him, or it) at liberty
v.t. 'To take by purchase, or for a price,' to buy, purchase.

ety: pass. part. of الزم 'to render necessary; — to convict,' &c., iv of لزِم; see lāzim
def: part. adj. Conjoined; annexed; driven together; closely joined or united; inseparable; — convinced, convicted; confuted; charged (with), accused (of), arraigned; sentenced, condemned; — blamable.
more: (part. pass. IV. of لزم) adj. 1. Convicted, convinced, confuted, blamable. 2. Conjoined, annexed, inseparable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملزم (mulzim), from Arabic مُلْزِم (mulzim).

ety: prop. مومأ mūmaʼ, pass. part. of اوْمأ 'to nod, beckon; to point to,' &c., iv of ومأ 'to make a sign'; see īmā
def: part. Pointed to, pointed out; alluded to; above-mentioned; assented to

ety: act. part. of ميّز 'to discriminate,' &c., ii of ماز (for ميز
def: part. adj. Separating, distinguishing; discriminating, discerning, judicious
more: part. act. ميز Distinguishing; discriminating. quvvat-i-mumaiyazah , n. f. The power of discrimination; judgment.
part. act. (II. of ميز) Distinguishing, separating, discriminating. ¶ mumaiyaz, part. pass. Separated, distinguished.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Separated, distinguished; discerned.

ety: for A. مميزة mumaiyizat, fem. of mumaiyiz
def: s.f. The discriminating power or faculty; judgment.

ety: fr. mumaiyiz, q.v.
def: s.f. Discrimination; judgment (i.q. tamīz).

ety: Prk. महुमक्खिआ; S. मधु+मक्षिका
def: s.f. (dialec.) A honey-bee (i.q. maumākhī).

ety: for A. ممانعة, inf. n. of مانع, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Preventing, forbidding, prohibiting; — prevention. prohibition, hinderance, restriction; obstruction, obstacle
more: n. f. منع forbade. Prohibition; restriction. mumāneat k. , v. a. To prohibit ( manā k .).
(inf. III. of منع Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. Prohibition, hinderance.

ety: fem. of māmū, q.v.; ānī = S. आनी
def: s.f. Mother's brother's wife, aunt (i.q. māmī, q.v.)
more: E. n. f. Mother's brother's wife ( māmī ); an aunt. Mūṅh par mumānī, pīṭh pīchhe gaibānī . Prov. Before my face 'Aunt,' behind my back 'thatharlot'!
s. f. An aunt, motherʼs brotherʼs wife. See مامي māmī.
subs: s.f. Husband's, or wife's, maternal aunt (i.q. mamiyā-sās).
wety: From مامُوں (māmū̃, “maternal uncle; mother's brother”) + ـَانی (-ānī).

ety: act. part., and n. of place, of مَاسَسَ, or ماسّ 'to be in contact with, to touch (another
def: part. adj. & s.m. Touching; tangent, or tangential; contiguous; — a tangent; — a place of touch or contact
more: n. f. مس Place of contact. mamās-ul-tamām ; H. sāth-chhūṭī lakīr ; S. कोटि स्यद्धिनी ( Geom .) Co-tangent. khat-i-mamās ; H. chhūtī lakīr . ( Geom .) A tangent.
(noun of place from مسّ Heb. and Chald. ) s. Place of contact, tangent.
subs: s.m. A touching line, a tangent (syn. ćhūtī lakīr).

ety: for A. مماسّة, inf. n. of ماسّ 'to touch'; see mumāss
def: s.f. Touching (another); lying with; — touch, contact; coition.
more: (inf. III. of مسّ) s. f. Touch.

ety: for A. مماطلة, inf. n. of ماطل 'to put off payment (of a debt
def: s.f. Putting off, deferring (payment of a debt, &c.); being dilatory (in performing a promise).

ety: act. part. of انبسط 'to be spread out; to be extended or dilated,' &c., vii of بسط 'to spread; to expand,' &c.
def: part. adj. Expanded; extended; dilated; — of an open, cheerful countenance; rejoicing, exulting, exultant.
more: (part. act. VII. of بسط) adj. Dilated, extended, rejoicing, exulting.

ety: act. part. of امدّ 'to extend, prolong; to assist,' &c.; iv of مدّ; see madd and madad
def: s.m. Extender, prolonger, protracter; — an assistant, a helper.
more: n. m. مد extending. A helper; an assistant ( sāthī ).
(part. act. IV. of مدّ) s. m. 1. An assistant. 2. An extender, protracter.

ety: prop. muʼmin, act. part. of آمن 'to render secure or safe (from
def: adj. & s.m. Believing (in God), faithful; orthodox; — a believer; an orthodox Muḥammadan (pl. mūminīn); — a Musalmān weaver.
more: n. m. 1. An orthodox Mahomedan. 2. A Mussalmān weaver.
(part. act. IV. of امن Heb. ) 1. adj. Orthodox, faithful, believing. 2. s. m. A musalmān weaver. (Plur. مومنين muminīn ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُؤْمِن (mu'min), borrowed from Arabic مُؤْمِن (muʔmin), from آمَنَ (ʔāmana). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi مومن (mvmn /⁠momin⁠/).

ety: mūmin, q.v.+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Faithful; orthodox (i.q. mūmin); — like a believer.
more: adj. Orthodox, faithful. adv. Like a believer.

ety: act. part. of امکن 'to give power; to be possible,' &c., iv of مکُن 'to hold rank or dignity,' &c.
def: adj. Possible, practicable, feasible; contingent; liable
more: adj. مکن being possible. Possible; practicable; feasible; can; liable; contingent. 1. Kyā mumkin jo zarā khabar tak lene āë? 2. Jiskā honā mumkin hai, uskā na honā bhī mumkin hai . What may be may not be. mumkin-ut-tabdīl ; H. palaṭne jog , adj. Commutable. mumkin-ut-taqsīm ; H. bāṅṭ-jog . adj. Partitionable; liable to partition. mumkin-ul-dakhūl , adj. Accessible. mumkin-uz-zirāٴat ; H. khetī-jog , adj Arable; culturable. mumkin-ul-vusūl, yā husūl ; H. pāne-jog . adj. Attainable. mumkin-ul-vuqū ; H. honhār , adj. 1. Possible. 2. What is to happen. mumkināt' , n. f. pl. Possibilities.
(part. act. IV. of مکن) adj. Possible. Plur. ممکنات mumkināt, Possibilities. ممکن الدخول mumkinu-d-duḵẖūl, Accessible.
subs: adj. Liable to partition; partitionable; — mumkinuʼl-ḥuṣūl, adj. Procurable; attainable
adj. Capable of cultivation, culturable; arable
adj. Possible to happen, possible; likely to arise; — what is to happen (syn. honhār).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ممکن (mumkin), from Arabic مُمْكِن (mumkin).

def: s.f. pl. (of mumkin), Possibilities; contingencies.

ety: act. part. of امسك 'to hold, hold fast, clutch,' &c., iv of مسك, idem
def: part. adj. & s.m. Holding; retaining, retentive; — economical; parsimonious, stingy, avaricious; — a miser, a niggard, a stingy fellow.
more: n. m. مسک holding fast. A miser; niggard. mum'sik , adj. 1. Retaining; retentive. 2. Stingy.
(part. act. IV. of مسک Heb. and Chald. ) 1. s. m. A miser, a niggard. 2. adj. Retaining, retentive.

ety: fr. mumsik
def: s.f. Parsimony, stinginess, avarice.

ety: pass. part. of اِمْتدّ 'to be extended,' &c. viii of مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: part. Extended, prolonged, protracted, stretched. ممتلی mumtalī (act. part. of اِمْتلأ 'to be filled,' &c., viii of ملأ 'to fill'), part. adj. Filled; replete; full; heaped up. ممتنع mumtaněʻ (act. part. of امتنع 'to be prohibited,' &c., viii of منع 'to prohibit,' &c.), part. adj. Prohibited, forbidden; impossible.
more: part. pass. (VIII. of مدّ Heb. and Aram. ) Extended, prolonged.

ety: act. part. of اِمتحن 'to examine, to try, prove,' &c., viii of محن 'to strike; to try, prove,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Examining, trying, proving; — examiner, tester, prover; auditor
more: n. m. محن striking. An examiner; auditor. mumtahin-i-kīmiyā , n. A chemical examiner. sarkārī mumtahin-i-kīmiyā , n. The chemical examiner to Government.
part. act. (VIII. of محن) Trying, proving, examining. ¶ mumtaḥan, part. pass. Tried, proved.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Examined, tested, tried, proved; experienced; — s.m. One who has been examined, &c.; an examinee

def: (part. pass. VIII. of منع Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Prohibited, forbidden, impossible.

ety: pass. part. of اِمتاز 'to be separated, be made distinct,' &c., viii of ماز (for
def: part. adj. Separated, or set apart (from), chosen, or selected (from); distinguished; select; exalted; illustrious, eminent, pre-eminent
more: adj. ميز distinguished. Exalted; eminent; illustrious. mumtāz k. , v. a. To exalt; elevate.
(part. pass. VIII. of ميز) adj. Distinguished; chosen, illustrious, exalted, eminent.
subs: v.t. To distinguish (a person, from, or from among, -se); to render pre-eminent, to exalt (one, above, -se)
(-meṅ), To be distinguished (in, or among); to be singled out for distinction (from among); to be pre-eminent (among); to be exalted (over, or above).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُمْتَاز (mumtāz), from Arabic مُمْتَاز (mumtāz).

def: adj. & s.m. Eager to be free (from mundane existence), striving after emancipation; — a sage abstracted from human passions, and striving after emancipation.

ety: fr. desid. of rt. मृ
def: adj. & s.m. Wishing to die; being about to die, being at the point of death; — a man at the point of death, a dying man.

s.m. = مني muni, q.v. s.m. An inspired saint, saint-sage, a holy man endowed with divine inspiration, or one who has attained more or less of a divine nature by mortification and abstraction; a pious and learned man; a sage, seer; a hermit, an ascetic, a devotee, a monk; — a term applied as a title to the Rishis and Brahmādiks, and to a great number of persons (as Agastya, Vyāsa, Pāṇini, &c.) distinguished for their writings and considered as inspired (seven of these Munis in particular are enumerated); — a symbolical expression for the number seven
subs: s.m. Dress of the Munīs (i.e. the bark of the Bhojpatr)
s.m. The duties that appertain to the status of a Muni
or (poet.) muni-rājū, or muni-rāyā, s.m. = munīśvar, q.v.
s.f. Daughter of a Muni or saint
s.m. The abode, or place of retirement of a recluse, or holy sage, &c.; a hermitage
s.m. An excellent sage, an eminent saint (i.q. muni-var, q.v.)
s.m. An epithet of respectful address to superiors
s.m. Best of ascetics or holy sages; an excellent Muni, an eminent saint; — an epithet of Śukrāćārya
s.m. An eminent ascetic or saint
vulg. munīs (˚ni+īśa), Chief of sages or ascetics; an eminent ascetic or saint
(˚ni+īś˚), s.f. Chief of sages or ascetics, &c. (i.q. munīś, q.v.); — an epithet of Vishnu, and of Buddha; — name of a commentator on the Siddhānta-Śiromaṇi
(˚ni+in˚), s.m. Chief of sages or ascetics (i.q. munīś); — an epithet of any Bauddha sage or teacher; and of Śākya-muni, and of Bharata, and of Śiva, and of a Dānava
s.m. The food of Munis or saints, &c. (consisting mostly of roots and fruits).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

adv. On waking, or rising (from bed)
v.n. 'The face to be bright or white,' not to incur disgrace or dishonour; to come off with credit, or with flying colours
adj. & adv. By word of mouth, verbal; — verbally (syn. zabānī)

v.n. 'The face to be soured,' to be displeased; — to have the taste blunted or depraved; — to have a bad taste in the mouth
To fashion the face (of, -kā); — to make faces (at), to mock; — to express feeling through the features
s.m. A muzzle; — adj. With the mouth closed
To hold one's tongue; — to stop the mouth (of, -kā), to silence; to give hush-money (to), to bribe (i.q. muṅh bharnā)

To make (oneself), or to put on, a good face; — to fit or qualify oneself (for)
adj. (f. -bolī), Nominal, in name or appellation, so-called; adopted

v.n. The month to water; — to feel nausea, to be squeamish

adv. 'Filling the mouth'; fully, completely, thoroughly (used by women; cf. peṭ-bhar-ke)
(-kā), To fill the mouth (of); to stop the mouth (of) with a sop, to give hush-money (to); to bribe

adv. On or upon (one's) mouth or face; — before the face, in the presence (of, -ke, or mere, &c.)
(-ke), To spit on the face (of); — (fig̠.) to disgrace, or insult
(-ke), To be restrained through respect or regard (for another)
'Not to put a grain to the mouth,' to go without a morsel of food
'A dove to fly across the face (of),' to change colour; to be or become confounded; to look blank or astounded
(-ke), To get warm or angry in the presence (of); to behave disrespectfully in the presence (of a superior)
To bring to the lips; to utter, say, speak of, tell, relate
To place a seal on the lips (of, -apne, or uske, &c.), to seal the lips
'To be without a nose on the face'; to be shameless

(kisī-ke), To fall into the mouth (of); — to be eaten; — to behave with boldness or presumption (before)
adj. f. (m. -paṛā), On the tongues (of people; — syn. zabān-zad)
v.n. To open the mouth wide, to gape (with wonder, &c.)
adj. & s.m. Outspoken, fearless, unconcerned; foul-mouthed, abusive; — an outspoken, or fearless, &c. person; — a vain babbler
v.n. The face to be turned or distorted, the face to be paralyzed; — the face to be turned away (from, -se), to be satiated or cloyed; to be disgusted (with); to be displeased (with)
To distend the cheeks; to make a wry face, &c. (i.q. muṅh bigāṛnā, q.v.)
'To burst open the lips and speak'; to break silence; to make bold to say
(-se, or -kī t̤araf-se), To turn the face away (from); to be averse (to); — to abstain (from); to avoid, shun
To gape (i.q. muṅh bānā, and muṅh pasārnā), to yawn; — to desire much; — to presume; — to demand a high price (for); to raise one's price

(-kā), To slap the face (of), to box the ears (of)
To distort the face; to make wry faces (see muṅh bigāṛnā)
'To join face (to face),' to draw close together and whisper; to talk scandal in an under-tone
(-kā), To lick the face (of); to caress; to make much (of), to curry favour (with), to toady
(-kā), To make mouths or faces (at), to mock; — to make a bad imitation (of), to caricature
adj. Attached (to), intimate (with); possessing the ear (of), having an ascendancy (over)
(-ke), To be or become familiar or intimate (with); to attach oneself (to); — to face, confront (with hostile intention)
To bite, to snap (as a horse); to open the mouth (to devour); — to work the mouth or jaws, to eat
The mouth to be moving or working; to chew the cud; — to be foul-mouthed
adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, bashful, timid; — keeping at home, not visiting friends
(-se), To hide or veil the face (from bashfulness, &c.); to keep parda; — to be bashful; — to be in hiding, to hide, skulk; — to avoid, shun, to evade
s.m. & f. One who hides his (or her) face; — a bashful person; — a skulker, &c.; — blind-man's buff
(-kā), To touch the face (of); — to give (one) a formal invitation (to)
adv. Face to face, before, in the presence (of)
(-ko), To show one's face (to), to appear before (one) with confidence and satisfaction (e.g. kyā muṅh dikhāʼūṅgā? 'how can I show my face?')
s.f. A present given (by the female relations of the bridegroom, and the friends of the family) on the first visit to a bride
(-ke, &c.), To continue staring at (one) with surprise, to be lost in astonishment
To say pleasant things (to), to flatter

(apnā, or kisī-kā, &c.), To see one's face (in a glass); — to look at the face (of); to look about idly; — to stare at in astonishment or helplessness; — to look to (one, for aid, &c.), to depend (on); — to have a regard (for one)
or prīt, or ćāh, or maḥabbat, Show of regard or friendship before a person; apparent friendship or affection
muṅh-dekhī bāt kahnā = muṅh dekh-kar bāt kahnā
(-meṅ), To put the mouth or lips (into, or to); — to make familiarized, to favour, to countenance
(-meṅ), To put the mouth (into, or to); to thrust the muzzle (into); to eat; — to bite (a horse); to peck or seize with the beak (a cock, in fighting); — to beg, request
To keep a good countenance; to keep on good terms (with)
adj. Verbal (message, or evidence, &c.), oral, vivâvoce
'The face to become yellow,' to turn pale (from fear)
Hard-mouthed (a horse); restive; — headstrong, stubborn, obstinate; — outspoken, &c. (i.q. muṅh-phaṭ, q.v.)
s.f. Hardness of mouth; headstrongness, &c.; — foul-mouthedness, abusiveness
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, to sulk; — to cause the face (of another, -kā, &c.) to swell (by a slap, or blow; — i.q. muṅh phulānā, and muṅh bigāṛnā)
To contract the face, to make a wry face
To bridle the mouth or tongue; to hold one's tongue
The mouth to become dry or parched; — the face to become thin or emaciated
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To take the words out of the mouth (of); to anticipate (and to prevent from speaking) one who is on the point of speaking
Flowers (of rhetoric) to fall from the lips (of, -ke); — (ironic.) to rate in round terms, to reproach, to abuse
Milk to drop from the mouth (of); to be yet a child, to have no sense
(-kā), To sew up the mouth (of); — to close or seal the lips (of, i.q. muṅh-par mǒhr karnā); to silence (a person); — to give hush-money (to, i.q. muṅh bharnā)
(-kā), To change colour, to turn pale (esp. from fear)
To blacken one's own face; to incur disgrace; — to blacken the face (of another, -kā); to disgrace, bring disgrace on; to punish; — to turn out with disgrace; to break off all connection (with)
The face to be or become black or blackened; to incur disgrace, be disgraced
muṅh-kā laććhan jhaṛnā = muṅh-kī loʼī utarnā
'With the mouth's (morsel) in the mouth and the hand's (spoonful) in the hand'; staggered, aghast
s.m. A morsel; — anything easily attained

(-kī t̤araf, or kī or), To turn the face (to or towards), to direct the steps (towards); to face, confront; — to attack; to abuse; — to regard with favour, be partial (to); — to direct the attention (to), to be greedy or miserly; to come to a head, to burst or, open (as a boil, or an abscess, &c.); — to make the first attempt at seizing game (a young hound, or other animal used in hunting)
(-ke), To get a taste for blood; vice to grow (on one)
The mouth to become open; — to become foul-mouthed or abusive
(-kā), To cause (one's) mouth to become opened; to make (or to provoke one to) speak
To open the mouth; — to abuse; — to uncover the face; to present to view; to reveal
(or bhal) girnā, To fall on the face, or face foremost
muṅh-ke kawwe uṛ-jānā = muṅh-par fāḵẖta uṛ-jānā
To eat one's own words; to be convicted out of one's own mouth; — to be browbeaten; — to receive a blow on the face; — to suffer disgrace; to be put to the blush
(-ke), To plug the mouth (of), to gag; to silence; — to tie the tongue (of a person) with a charm or spell; — to exorcise
or jānā, To lose (one's) brightness of countenance, or (one's) good looks; — to lose the sense of shame; — to lose (one's) good manners, to become corrupted, or depraved
To redden the mouth or lips; — to make the face (of another, -kā) red (by slapping)
v.n. The mouth or lips to be tinged with red; — the face to become red, or to flush (with anger, &c.); — to be honoured, to get a good name
'To muffle up the face and lie down'; to lie down in the sulks; to take to one's bed in grief
To hang down the face or head; — to make a long face, to be down in the mouth
To apply the mouth or lips (to, -meṅ)

(-ke, or mere, &c.), To have the mouth burned or affected (by a pungent substance, &c.); — to suit the mouth or palate (of), to be agreeable to the taste (of); — to become used, or accustomed (to), or familiarized (with); — to become intimate (with), to become a favourite (of); — to dispute, or bandy words (with)
'To attack (one) with the mouth,' to bite; to sting; to say biting or stinging things; — to attack, to fall upon; — to stop the mouth (of a person, -kā); to silence; — to feed
or muṅh-māgā, adj. (f. -ī), Demanded, requested, or asked for by word of mouth
s.m. Price demanded or asked for; — muṅh-māṅgī murād, s.f. Any thing or object obtained in accordance with expressed wish; — heart-felt wish
s.f. The death asked or wished for
To make a wry face; — to turn up the nose; — to frown, to be displeased, or offended

(-se), To turn away the face (from); to disregard; to be averse (to); to avoid, shun; to abstain or desist (from); — to turn (against), to rebel, revolt; — to turn a deaf ear (to); to give the cold shoulder (to)
(-kā), To sweeten the mouth (of); to give (one) a treat; — to give a sop or bribe (to)
The mouth to water; to feel eager desire (for); — to be tempted, or seduced
(-ke), To drop water into the mouth (of); — to perform the last offices (for)
To read to oneself, to mumble
To take a straw in one's mouth (as a sign of humility, or submission); to humble or abase oneself, to lick the dust; — to submit (to), resign oneself (to)
s.m.f.(?) The mouth-piece of a ḥuqqa
To look blank or foolish; — to simper
v.n. Not to have the face (to)
v.n. The cheeks to sink, &c. (i.q. muṅh utar-jānā, q.v.)
'The face and hands to break'; the limbs to be broken; to meet with a bad accident
To wash the face and hands
To return (from any enterprise) with a face (expressive of disappointment) peculiarly one's own; to return disappointed; to fail of success
To put the hand to the mouth (in admiration, or astonishment, or shame) and remain motionless; to be astonished, or ashamed, &c.
or ḵẖāk tere muṅh-meṅ, Dirt in your mouth! may your mouth be fouled!
(May his, or your,) face (become) black! may disgrace overtake him!
