To distort the face; to make wry faces (see muṅh bigāṛnā)
'To join face (to face),' to draw close together and whisper; to talk scandal in an under-tone
(-kā), To lick the face (of); to caress; to make much (of), to curry favour (with), to toady
(-kā), To make mouths or faces (at), to mock; — to make a bad imitation (of), to caricature
adj. Attached (to), intimate (with); possessing the ear (of), having an ascendancy (over)
(-ke), To be or become familiar or intimate (with); to attach oneself (to); — to face, confront (with hostile intention)
To bite, to snap (as a horse); to open the mouth (to devour); — to work the mouth or jaws, to eat
The mouth to be moving or working; to chew the cud; — to be foul-mouthed
adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, bashful, timid; — keeping at home, not visiting friends
(-se), To hide or veil the face (from bashfulness, &c.); to keep parda; — to be bashful; — to be in hiding, to hide, skulk; — to avoid, shun, to evade
s.m. & f. One who hides his (or her) face; — a bashful person; — a skulker, &c.; — blind-man's buff
(-kā), To touch the face (of); — to give (one) a formal invitation (to)
adv. Face to face, before, in the presence (of)
(-ko), To show one's face (to), to appear before (one) with confidence and satisfaction (e.g. kyā muṅh dikhāʼūṅgā? 'how can I show my face?')
s.f. A present given (by the female relations of the bridegroom, and the friends of the family) on the first visit to a bride
(-ke, &c.), To continue staring at (one) with surprise, to be lost in astonishment
To say pleasant things (to), to flatter

(apnā, or kisī-kā, &c.), To see one's face (in a glass); — to look at the face (of); to look about idly; — to stare at in astonishment or helplessness; — to look to (one, for aid, &c.), to depend (on); — to have a regard (for one)
or prīt, or ćāh, or maḥabbat, Show of regard or friendship before a person; apparent friendship or affection
muṅh-dekhī bāt kahnā = muṅh dekh-kar bāt kahnā
(-meṅ), To put the mouth or lips (into, or to); — to make familiarized, to favour, to countenance
(-meṅ), To put the mouth (into, or to); to thrust the muzzle (into); to eat; — to bite (a horse); to peck or seize with the beak (a cock, in fighting); — to beg, request
To keep a good countenance; to keep on good terms (with)
adj. Verbal (message, or evidence, &c.), oral, vivâvoce
'The face to become yellow,' to turn pale (from fear)
Hard-mouthed (a horse); restive; — headstrong, stubborn, obstinate; — outspoken, &c. (i.q. muṅh-phaṭ, q.v.)
s.f. Hardness of mouth; headstrongness, &c.; — foul-mouthedness, abusiveness
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, to sulk; — to cause the face (of another, -kā, &c.) to swell (by a slap, or blow; — i.q. muṅh phulānā, and muṅh bigāṛnā)
To contract the face, to make a wry face
To bridle the mouth or tongue; to hold one's tongue
The mouth to become dry or parched; — the face to become thin or emaciated
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To take the words out of the mouth (of); to anticipate (and to prevent from speaking) one who is on the point of speaking
Flowers (of rhetoric) to fall from the lips (of, -ke); — (ironic.) to rate in round terms, to reproach, to abuse
Milk to drop from the mouth (of); to be yet a child, to have no sense
(-kā), To sew up the mouth (of); — to close or seal the lips (of, i.q. muṅh-par mǒhr karnā); to silence (a person); — to give hush-money (to, i.q. muṅh bharnā)
(-kā), To change colour, to turn pale (esp. from fear)
To blacken one's own face; to incur disgrace; — to blacken the face (of another, -kā); to disgrace, bring disgrace on; to punish; — to turn out with disgrace; to break off all connection (with)
The face to be or become black or blackened; to incur disgrace, be disgraced
muṅh-kā laććhan jhaṛnā = muṅh-kī loʼī utarnā
'With the mouth's (morsel) in the mouth and the hand's (spoonful) in the hand'; staggered, aghast
s.m. A morsel; — anything easily attained

(-kī t̤araf, or kī or), To turn the face (to or towards), to direct the steps (towards); to face, confront; — to attack; to abuse; — to regard with favour, be partial (to); — to direct the attention (to), to be greedy or miserly; to come to a head, to burst or, open (as a boil, or an abscess, &c.); — to make the first attempt at seizing game (a young hound, or other animal used in hunting)
(-ke), To get a taste for blood; vice to grow (on one)
The mouth to become open; — to become foul-mouthed or abusive
(-kā), To cause (one's) mouth to become opened; to make (or to provoke one to) speak
To open the mouth; — to abuse; — to uncover the face; to present to view; to reveal
(or bhal) girnā, To fall on the face, or face foremost
muṅh-ke kawwe uṛ-jānā = muṅh-par fāḵẖta uṛ-jānā
To eat one's own words; to be convicted out of one's own mouth; — to be browbeaten; — to receive a blow on the face; — to suffer disgrace; to be put to the blush
(-ke), To plug the mouth (of), to gag; to silence; — to tie the tongue (of a person) with a charm or spell; — to exorcise
or jānā, To lose (one's) brightness of countenance, or (one's) good looks; — to lose the sense of shame; — to lose (one's) good manners, to become corrupted, or depraved
To redden the mouth or lips; — to make the face (of another, -kā) red (by slapping)
v.n. The mouth or lips to be tinged with red; — the face to become red, or to flush (with anger, &c.); — to be honoured, to get a good name
'To muffle up the face and lie down'; to lie down in the sulks; to take to one's bed in grief
To hang down the face or head; — to make a long face, to be down in the mouth
To apply the mouth or lips (to, -meṅ)

(-ke, or mere, &c.), To have the mouth burned or affected (by a pungent substance, &c.); — to suit the mouth or palate (of), to be agreeable to the taste (of); — to become used, or accustomed (to), or familiarized (with); — to become intimate (with), to become a favourite (of); — to dispute, or bandy words (with)
'To attack (one) with the mouth,' to bite; to sting; to say biting or stinging things; — to attack, to fall upon; — to stop the mouth (of a person, -kā); to silence; — to feed
or muṅh-māgā, adj. (f. -ī), Demanded, requested, or asked for by word of mouth
s.m. Price demanded or asked for; — muṅh-māṅgī murād, s.f. Any thing or object obtained in accordance with expressed wish; — heart-felt wish
s.f. The death asked or wished for
To make a wry face; — to turn up the nose; — to frown, to be displeased, or offended

(-se), To turn away the face (from); to disregard; to be averse (to); to avoid, shun; to abstain or desist (from); — to turn (against), to rebel, revolt; — to turn a deaf ear (to); to give the cold shoulder (to)
(-kā), To sweeten the mouth (of); to give (one) a treat; — to give a sop or bribe (to)
The mouth to water; to feel eager desire (for); — to be tempted, or seduced
(-ke), To drop water into the mouth (of); — to perform the last offices (for)
To read to oneself, to mumble
To take a straw in one's mouth (as a sign of humility, or submission); to humble or abase oneself, to lick the dust; — to submit (to), resign oneself (to)
s.m.f.(?) The mouth-piece of a ḥuqqa
To look blank or foolish; — to simper
v.n. Not to have the face (to)
v.n. The cheeks to sink, &c. (i.q. muṅh utar-jānā, q.v.)
'The face and hands to break'; the limbs to be broken; to meet with a bad accident
To wash the face and hands
To return (from any enterprise) with a face (expressive of disappointment) peculiarly one's own; to return disappointed; to fail of success
To put the hand to the mouth (in admiration, or astonishment, or shame) and remain motionless; to be astonished, or ashamed, &c.
or ḵẖāk tere muṅh-meṅ, Dirt in your mouth! may your mouth be fouled!
(May his, or your,) face (become) black! may disgrace overtake him!

ety: mū˚ = Prk. मुअ(इ), fr. मुअ = S. मृत p.p.p. of rt. मृ
def: v.n. To die, expire.
more: see مرنا) v. n. To die.

ety: pass. part. of نبّت 'to cause to grow, or to grow out,' ii of نبت 'to grow'
def: part. adj. & s.m. 'Caused to grow out or to be raised'; ornamented in relief, embossed; inlaid; — raised work; embossment; inlaid work
more: (part. pass. II. of نبت) adj. Inlaid. منبت کار munabbat-kār, An inlayer. منبت کاري munabbat-kār-ī, s. f. Inlaying.

def: adj. = munabbat; — s.f. = munabbat-kārī.

ety: pass. part. of نادَي; see munādī
def: part. Called, &c.; — s.m. The person called or addressed; (in Gram.) the vocative case.

ety: for A. منادٍ munādiṉ, act. part. of نادَي 'to call to; to proclaim,' &c., iii of ندا (for ندو
def: s.m. A crier, herald, proclaimer; a preacher.

ety: act. part. of اِنْعدم 'to become non-existent,' &c., vii of عدم 'to be wanting; not to exist,' &c.
def: part. adj. Non-existent; extinct; missing; lost; destroyed, annihilated.
more: (part. act. VII. of عدم) adj. Annihilated, destroyed, extinct.

ety: act. part. of نافق 'to act hypocritically,' &c., iii of نفق 'to be saleable,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Hypocritical; atheistic; impious; — a hypocrite, dissembler; an infidel; an atheist; — an enemy.
more: n. m. نفق 1. A hypocrite. 2. An infidel; atheist.
(part. act. III. of نفق Chald. and Syr. ) s. m. A hypocrite, an atheist, an enemy.

ety: pass. part. of نغّص 'to render turbid, or disturbed; to render wretched, or miserable,' &c., ii of نغص 'to be turbid,' &c.
def: part. adj. Disturbed (in mind), rendered miserable; miserable, wretched, sad, melancholy (cf. malīn, and man-malīn).
more: (part. pass. II. of نغص) adj. Miserable, melancholy.

ety: prob. S. मुख्य+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Chief, head.
more: adj. Chief; head. Munāh munāh-ke log jamā hue .

ety: for A. مناجاة, inf. n. of ناجَي 'to act, or to speak, privately with (another
def: s.f. Prayer, supplication
more: n. m. 1. Prayers; supplications. 2. Hymns. munājat paṛhnā , v. n. To read prayers.
(inf. III. of نجو) s. f. Prayer, supplication.
subs: To repeat prayers, to say (one's) prayers.

def: s. One who prays.

ety: act. part. of نجّم 'to prognosticate or calculate (any event
def: s.m. An astronomer; astrologer; almanac-maker (P. pl. munajjimān; Ar. obl. pl. munajjimīn).
more: n. m. نجم a star, planet. 1. An astrologer. 2. An almanack-maker.
(part. act. II. of نجم) s. m. An astronomer, an astrologer, an almanack-maker.

ety: prob. a corr. form; cf. manqabat
def: s.f. Virtue; ability; praise.
more: (inf. III. of نقب Chald. and Aram. ) s. f. Virtue, ability, praise.

ety: for A. مناقشة munāqashat, inf. n. of ناقش 'to enter minutely into calculation with; to quarrel with,' &c., iii of نقش; see naqsh
def: s.m. Quarrelling, wrangling (with, -se); — contention, dispute, quarrel.
more: (inf. III. of نقش) s. m. Contention, dispute, wrangling.

ety: act. part. of اِنْعقد 'to become tied, or knit,' &c., vii of عقد 'to tie,' &c.
def: part. adj. Tied, knit; bound (by contract, &c.); — agreed upon, covenanted; concluded, settled, established; organized; confirmed, ratified (said of a sale or bargain, a contract, or a marriage, &c.); — held, celebrated
more: adj. عقد tied. 1. Bound (by contract, etc.). 2. Held; celebrated. Majlis munaqad huī .
(part. act. VII. of عقد) adj. Tied, bound, covenanted, established.

ety: act. part. of انعکس 'to become inverted, or reversed,' &c., vii (and quasi-pass.
def: part. adj. Inverted, inversed, reversed, reflected (as a figure in a mirror or water); turned upside down, topsy-turvy; — inverse; contrary.
more: (part. act. VII. of عکس Heb. ) adj. Inverse, inverted, reversed, topsyturvy, turned downward.

def: s.m. = منقي munaqqā, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A species of raisins, currants ( Harris ): see منقيٰ munaḳḳạ̄, sign. 2.

ety: pass. part. of نقّي 'to clean, clear,' &c., ii of نقي 'to be pure or clean'
def: part. adj. Cleaned, cleared, purged, purified; — s.m. A species of dried grapes or raisins; bloom-raisins.
more: n. f. lit . cleared of stone, used for mavīz . Bloom raisins.
(part. pass. II. of نقي Heb. and Chald. ) 1. adj. Cleaned, purged, purified. 2. s. m. A species of raisin.

ety: pass. part. of نقّح 'to lop or cut off; to clean, clear,' &c., ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Made manifest, or clear; cleared (of weak words a verse, &c.).
more: (part. pass. II. of نقح) adj. Made manifest or clear, cleared of weak words (a verse).

ety: pass. part. of نقّش, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Painted, coloured; stained; printed, speckled; variegated; — embroidered; — carved; engraved.
more: (part. pass. II. of نقش) adj. Painted, embroidered, engraven, carved. ¶ minḳash (noun of instrum.) s. Pincers, tweezers.

def: s.m. (dialec.) The pheasant of the Himalayas.

ety: for A. مناسبة, inf. n. of ناسب 'to be similar, or like,' &c., iii of نسب 'to refer (to
def: s.f. Relation, connexion; proportion; analogy; comparison; — suitableness, fitness; propriety, aptness; — accordance, correspondence, consistence; expediency; convenience
more: n. f. نسب 1. Relation; connection ( lāg , 1). 2. Suitableness; fitness.
subs: (-se), To bear relation (to); to be proportionate (to); — to be suitable, or adapted (to); to correspond, to accord (with), &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُنَاسَبَت (munāsabat), from Arabic مُنَاسَبَة (munāsaba).

ety: act. part. of ناسب; see munāsabat
def: adj. Related (to); — corresponding; adapted (to); conformable: agreeing (with); apposite; analogous; congruous; just, fit, convenient, proper, suitable, applicable, becoming, expedient, pertinent
more: adj. نسب Suitable (اُچت). munāsib jānnā, yā samajhnā , v. n. To consider proper or suitable. munāsib nahīṅ . Inapplicable. munāsib, yā shāyāṅ h . To apply; suit.
(part. act. III. of نسب Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Proper, convenient, fit, meet, suitable, becoming, expedient, pertinent, congruous, correspondent, apposite.
(inf. III. of نسب) s. f. Suitableness, proportion, connexion, relation, accordance, correspondence, consistency, expediency, agreeableness, analogy, comparison.
subs: or munāsib samajhnā, v.t. To consider as just, fit, or proper, &c.
It is not suitable, or applicable, or proper, or expedient, &c.
v.n. To be proper, &c.; to suit; to apply.
ex: خوشگوار و مُناسِب ہے وطن خاطر مرنا۔
xuśgwār ō munāsib hai watan xātir marna.
It is pleasant and appropriate to die for one's homeland.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُنَاسِب (munāsib).

ety: fr. munāsib
def: s.f. Suitableness, fitness, propriety, &c. (i.q. munāsabat, q.v.). s.f. Relation, connexion; proportion; analogy; comparison; — suitableness, fitness; propriety, aptness; — accordance, correspondence, consistence; expediency; convenience
subs: (-se), To bear relation (to); to be proportionate (to); — to be suitable, or adapted (to); to correspond, to accord (with), &c.

ety: for A. مونة mūnat; prop. مؤنة maʼunat, fr. مأن 'to make provision (for
def: s.f. Provisions; daily food.
more: (or maunat, from مأن) s. f. Provisions, daily food.

ety: pass. part. of نوّر 'to illuminate,' &c., ii of نار (for نور
def: part. adj. Illuminated, enlightened; elucidated, explained, illustrated; clear, bright, brilliant, splendid.
more: adj. نور light. 1. Illuminated; brilliant. 2. Enlightened.
(part. pass. II. of نور Heb. ) adj. Illustrated, explained, illuminated, enlightened, clear, splendid, brilliant.

ety: for A. منازعة, inf. n. of نازع 'to contend (against
def: s.f. Contention, contest, fierce strife, altercation; controversy; litigation.
more: (inf. III. of نزع) s. f. Contest, controversy, litigation, altercation.

ety: for A. مناظرة munāz̤arat, inf. n. of ناظر 'to dispute with,' &c., iii of نظر 'to turn the eyes (to
def: s.m. Disputation, dispute, discussion, debate, argumentation, argument; contest, struggle, strife.
more: n. m. نظر looking closely. Disputation; discussion; debate.
(inf. III. of نظر Heb. and Aram. and ) s. m. Disputation, dispute, discussion, contest, argumentation, argument.

ety: pass. part. of نزّه 'to keep oneself at a distance (from
def: part. adj. Kept apart (from impurity, &c.), pure, spotless, blameless, holy; — exempted (from), free.
more: (part. pass. II. of نزه) adj. 1. Kept apart from all impurity, pure, blameless, holy. 2. Exempted from, free.

ety: dialec.
def: s.f. = موچهہ mūćh,
s.f. (dialec.) = mūćh, or moćh, q.v. s.f. Moustache; — whisker; — (met.) boasting, boast, brag
subs: 'A moustache-twirling bread-breaker,' one who is proud of eating the bread of idleness
s.m. One who shaves his moustache, or whose moustache is shaved off
(or mūćhoṅ-ko) tāʼo denā, To twirl or twist the moustache; to act the fop or swell, to be proud or vain; to boast
To pass the hand over, or to stroke, the moustache; to be proud or vain, &c. (i.q. mūćhoṅ-ko tāʼo denā)
To make an offering on the appearance of the moustache (of a son, — the offering is made by the parents, among Muḥammadans)
(-kī), To cause the moustaches to be plucked out (a mode of punishment formerly practised in India)
To turn or twist the moustaches upwards; to be vain, &c. (i.q. mūćhoṅ-ko tāʼo denā).

ety: dialec.
def: s.f. = موچهہ mūćh,
s. f. Whiskers. See موچهـ.
(dakh.) v. a. To close, stop up, shut: see موچنا mūchnā, sign. 3.

adj. (f. -ā), Shaved, bald, having no hair on the head; uncovered; — destitute of horns (an ox, or a ram), hornless (i.q. muṇḍā, q.v.); — stripped of top-leaves or branches, lopped (as a tree); pointless, blunt; (fig.) dull, obtuse; — low, mean; — s.m. A shaven head, a bald pate; a skull; — the forehead; — the head; — head, headman, chief, principal (i.q. murh, q.v.); — a tree stripped of its top branches, the trunk of a lopped tree; a pollard; — a barber; — an epithet of Rāhu; — name of a Daitya
more: s. m. 1. The head. منڐ مال munḍ-māl, A necklace of human heads. 2. A head man. ¶ adj. (dakh.) Blunt, dull, obtuse. ( Harris ). منڐ پنا munḍ-panā, s. m. Dulness, bluntness: see, also, سرکشي sar-kash-ī. मंड manḍ, s. m. See مانڐ mānḍ.
subs: (˚ḍa+ak˚), s.m. Headless letters, the Nāgarī character without the top line (as written by the Kāyaths; see kaithi, and muṛiyā)
s.m. A sect of mendicants who extort alms by threatening to split (or wound) their heads, &c.; an individual of that sect; an extortioner
s.m. The action, or the quality, of a muṇḍ-cirā; extortion

s.f. A posture in which the legs are drawn up and the head is thrust either between them, or into some corner or recess, or under some covering or covert; crouching or contracted form of the body and limbs
To lie with the head between the legs, &c.; to lie round with the knees to the stomach, and the head between them, or resting on them
or muṇḍ-mālā, s.f. A necklace or string of human skulls.

ety: Prk. मुंडं; S. मुण्डं
def: s.m. The head; — (dialec.) a square cloth of three or four cubits, as a light turban or wrapper for the head, or worn round the pelvis
more: s. m. The head. مونڐ بهيڙا mūnḍ-bheṛā, Butting (as rams). مونڐ پانا mūnḍ pānā, To coax, to wheedle. ¶ A cloth three cubits long, worn round the pelvis. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: or mūṅḍ-bheṛā, s.m. 'Striking with the head,' butting
(-kā), To get the ear (of); to get round (a person); to coax; to wheedle
s.m. A kind of pillar or column
To beat the head (in perplexity, or trouble, &c.); to rack the brains; to make strenuous effort; — to throw (a thing) at the head (of, -ke)
To get the head shaved; — to become a bairāgī, or a sanyāsī, &c.

ety: fr. mūṅdnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Shutting, closing, fastening, &c.
more: n. m. mūṅḍī, muṅḍiyā , n. f. S. मुण्ड The head. Saukan pāṛe jhūṅḍ, chhet gaū kā mūṅḍ . Rus. Her hair is by her co-wife pulled, her blows fall on the ox's head. mūṅḍ mārnā , v. a. See sir mārnā , and sir se mārnā . To fling or cast at one's head. mūṅḍ muṅḍānā , v. a. 1. See sir muṅḍānā , 2. To be shaven and shorn. 1. Mūṅḍ muṅḍāë, jaṭā rakhāë, nagan phire jūṅ bhaiṅsā, Khalṛī ūpar rākh lagāë, man jaise kā taisā . Kabīr. With shaven head and hair grown long as buffalos naked stray, Their skins all over rubbed with dust, their hearts no good betray. 2. Ghar meṅ rahe na tīrath gaë, mūṅḍ muṅḍā pha- dīhat bhaë . E. Rus. Prov. Nor stayed at home, nor pilgrimage he made, shaven and shorn with shame he was o'erlaid. 3. Je barn adham telī, kumhārā, Svapach, kirāt, kol, kalvārā, Nārī muī ghar, sampati nāsī, Mūṅḍ muṅḍāë bhaye Sanyāsī . Rāmāyan. Low castes, as oilmen, potters, and the ráce Of sweepers,…

ety: Prk. मुंडओ and मुंडअं; S. मुण्डकः and मुण्डकं
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Shaven; bald; uncovered; having no horns, hornless (an ox, a ram, &c.); stripped of top leaves or branches, lopped (as a tree); docked; headless, topless; without a pinnacle (a mosque, &c.); having no point, not pointed (a shoe, &c.); — s.m. The head; — the head (of a paper kite); — the Kaithī or Muriyā character or writing (i.q. muṇḍākshar, q.v.s.v. muṇḍ); — a shaveling; — a disciple (syn. ćelā); — (dialec.) a boy (lit. 'shaveling'); — a kind of uncovered pālkī; — a boot.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa).

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of moṅḍhā, 'shoulder,' q.v. s.m. A kind of seat, or stool (made of reeds, or cane, and cord); a footstool; — the shoulder (i.q. muḍḍhā); a hump; a lump; — a gusset on the shoulder of a coat; — mouth (of a bag); — a sprout or shoot (of sugar-cane).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa).

def: s.f. (of muṇḍa or muṇḍ), A close-shaved, or bald-headed, woman or girl; a female mendicant; — Bengal madder, Rubia manjeth.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa).

ety: perf. part. of mūṅdnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Shut, closed; covered; fastened; involved; imprisoned.

ety: muṇḍā(nā) + āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ) तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. The act of shaving; — fee for shaving (i.q. muṅṛāʼī).
s.m. (f. -ikā), A shaver, a barber.
wety: From Sanskrit मण्डूक (maṇḍūka).
